cards.back: A bipartite graph is a graph whose vertices can be divided into two disjoint sets U and V such that every edge connects a vertex in U to one in V.

cards.front: What is the degree of a vertex in a graph?
cards.back: The degree of a vertex in a graph is the number of edges incident on it.

cards.front: What happens when a bipartite graph has one vertex with no edges?
cards.back: A vertex with no edges in a bipartite graph is known as an isolated vertex.

cards.front: Can a bipartite graph have an isolated vertex?
cards.back: Yes, a bipartite graph can have an isolated vertex. In this case, the isolated vertex will not be connected to any other vertex in the graph.

cards.front: Is an isolated vertex in a bipartite graph always a vertex in one of the disjoint sets U or V?
cards.back: Yes, in a bipartite graph, an isolated vertex will always be a vertex in one of the disjoint sets U or V.
